news.headline: ClearPoint Neuro (CLPT) surges 26.0%: Is this an indication of further gains?
news.summary: A Zacks momentum piece flagged CLPT's outsized rally, tying the move to sustained investor enthusiasm after partner uniQure's FDA progress on AMT-130 and rising earnings-estimate revisions. The note frames the stock as riding platform-validation momentum rather than a fresh operational catalyst, so continued gains hinge on the uniQure BLA timeline holding.

news.headline: ClearPoint Neuro CFO and CBO add ESPP shares via Form 4
news.summary: The CFO acquired 1,085 shares and the CBO added 1,781 shares (now holding 110,263) through the employee stock purchase plan in early July. The insider accumulation is a modest alignment signal following the mid-June AMT-130 rally, though ESPP buys are routine and lower-conviction than open-market purchases.

news.headline: ClearPoint Neuro CEO exercises options and sells 20,000 shares under 10b5-1 plan
news.summary: CEO Joe Burnett exercised options and sold 20,000 shares under a pre-arranged 10b5-1 trading plan, following a larger 61,843-share option-related sale on June 22. The scheduled nature of the disposals tempers the read, but the string of CEO sales into strength is a mild governance watch-item against the otherwise bullish insider ESPP activity.

news.headline: Lake Street maintains ClearPoint Neuro at Buy, raises target to $30
news.summary: Lake Street reaffirmed its Buy rating and the Street-high $30 price target, framing ClearPoint as the primary picks-and-shovels supplier benefiting from uniQure's AMT-130 progress. The call reflects growing analyst conviction post-FDA news but leaves valuation heavily dependent on partner execution.

news.headline: uniQure plans Q3 2026 BLA for AMT-130 after FDA accepts 3-year data for accelerated approval
news.summary: In a Type B meeting the FDA said the 3-year Phase I/II analysis (showing a 75% slowing of disease progression at the high dose) is acceptable as the primary basis for a BLA under accelerated approval for AMT-130 in Huntington's disease, with submission planned for Q3 2026. AMT-130 is delivered directly into the brain via ClearPoint's SmartFlow cannula and navigation platform, so CLPT benefits directly; the stock surged 26-32% intraday, the single biggest catalyst of the period.

news.headline: ClearPoint enters 10-year focused-ultrasound partnership with Sungkyunkwan University, South Korea
news.summary: The decade-long collaboration develops focused ultrasound for blood-brain-barrier opening, expanding ClearPoint's drug-delivery ecosystem and deepening its footprint in Asia. It is a strategic, long-dated bet that broadens the addressable market beyond MRI-guided direct injection, with limited near-term revenue impact.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings (after market close)
catalysts.detail: Next quarterly report; watch organic growth, IRRAflow integration, cash burn and confirmation of the $52-56M FY guide. Date follows the historical mid-August cadence and is not yet formally confirmed.

catalysts.label: uniQure AMT-130 BLA submission
catalysts.detail: uniQure plans to file the BLA for AMT-130 in Huntington's under accelerated approval in Q3 2026; ClearPoint's SmartFlow cannula and navigation platform is the delivery system, making this the key partner milestone. FDA also wants confirmatory-study design alignment before filing.

catalysts.label: Potential FDA decision on AMT-130
catalysts.detail: Assuming a Q3 2026 filing and priority/rolling review, an FDA decision could arrive around mid-2027; approval of a first Huntington's gene therapy would establish recurring per-procedure delivery revenue for ClearPoint.

catalysts.label: Revenue guidance $52-56M
catalysts.detail: Management's full-year target implies continued double-digit growth; meeting or raising it at the Q2/Q3 prints is viewed as a share-price catalyst.

catalysts.label: Velocity Alpha drill / navigation rollout
catalysts.detail: Continued commercialization of the CE-marked, FDA-cleared Velocity Alpha MR high-speed surgical drill and navigation systems into new hospital sites to drive hardware and recurring consumable placements.

pulse: CLPT's setup remains driven almost entirely by partner uniQure's mid-June regulatory win: the FDA's acceptance of 3-year AMT-130 data for an accelerated-approval BLA (targeted Q3 2026) sent the stock up 26-32% and revived the picks-and-shovels bull case, with Zacks momentum coverage keeping it in focus into July. Analysts are constructive (Lake Street Street-high at $30), but the operating picture is partner-dependent and unprofitable, so upside rests on gene therapies reaching market rather than standalone fundamentals. Insider signals are mixed - CFO/CBO ESPP buying against repeated scheduled CEO option sales - and the key near-term watch items are the ~Aug 11 Q2 print and confirmation of the actual uniQure BLA filing; between partner headlines the stock tends to consolidate.
